name: Harbor Street Alley
description: A narrow passage between a chandler's shop and a sailmaker's loft, running from King Street down to the waterfront — it is the kind of place where men go when they do not want to be seen, and tonight it smells of tobacco and recent footsteps in the mud. A single lantern at the far end casts more shadow than light.
atmosphericDetails: Fresh boot prints in the mud lead toward the waterfront — two sets, one larger than the other, walking close together.
A torn scrap of paper is caught on a nail in the wall — it bears a partial column of numbers in a cramped, precise hand.
The sound of the crowd at Old South echoes strangely down the alley, making it impossible to tell how far away the meeting house is.
